sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.12 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let us say that a group$G$has the property (u.-$n$-p.) if for every$n$-tuple of finite nonempty subsets$A_1, , A_n G$there exists$g G$which can be written in one and only one way as$g = a_1 a_n$with$a_i A_i$. It is easy to see that u.-$n$-p. implies u.-$m$-p. for$n m$(since some of the$A_i$can be$\{1\}$). Are the conditions u.-$n$-p. ($n 2$) all equivalent to u.-2-p., the usual unique product cond...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
